stem: For which value of the constant \( c \) is \( f(x) = c\,x^{[[m]]} \) a valid probability density function on the interval \( 0 \le x \le [[b]] \) (and \( f(x) = 0 \) elsewhere)?
distractor_rationales: This uses \( [[b]]^{[[m]]} \) instead of \( [[b]]^{[[m]]+1} \) in the denominator (forgot to raise the exponent from integrating).
This uses \( [[m]] \) instead of \( [[m]]+1 \) in the numerator.
This drops the \( ([[m]]+1) \) factor from the numerator entirely.
This is the value of the integral \( \int_0^{[[b]]} x^{[[m]]}\,dx \), i.e. \( 1/c \), not \( c \) itself.
This is off by one in the numerator, using \( [[m]]+2 \).
This divides by \( [[b]] \) instead of \( [[b]]^{[[m]]+1} \).

stem: For which value of the constant \( c \) is \( f(x) = c \) (a constant function) a valid probability density function on the interval \( [[[a]], [[b]]] \) (and \( f(x) = 0 \) elsewhere)?
solution: A constant density on \( [a,b] \) must satisfy \( c(b-a) = 1 \), so \( c = \frac{1}{b-a} = \frac{1}{[[b]] - [[a]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reports the interval width itself, i.e. \( 1/c \), rather than \( c \).
This adds the endpoints instead of subtracting them.
This halves the width instead of taking its reciprocal.
This has an extra factor of 2 in the numerator.
This incorrectly splits the reciprocal across the two endpoints.
This is off by one in the width.

stem: For which value of the constant \( c \) is \( f(x) = c\,([[b]] - x) \) a valid probability density function on the interval \( 0 \le x \le [[b]] \) (and \( f(x) = 0 \) elsewhere)?
solution: A density must integrate to 1: \( \int_0^{[[b]]} c([[b]]-x)\,dx = c\,\frac{[[b]]^2}{2} = 1 \), so \( c = \frac{2}{[[b]]^2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the factor of 2 from the numerator, using \( 1/b^2 \) instead of \( 2/b^2 \).
This forgets to square \( b \), using \( 2/b \) instead of \( 2/b^2 \).
This is the value of the integral itself, i.e. \( 1/c \), not \( c \).
This has an extra factor of 2 in the denominator.
This uses 4 instead of 2 in the numerator.
This subtracts 1 after computing the correct ratio.

stem: For which value of the constant \( c \) is \( f(x) = c\,x\,([[b]] - x) \) a valid probability density function on the interval \( 0 \le x \le [[b]] \) (and \( f(x) = 0 \) elsewhere)?
solution: A density must integrate to 1: \( \int_0^{[[b]]} c\,x([[b]]-x)\,dx = c\,\frac{[[b]]^3}{6} = 1 \), so \( c = \frac{6}{[[b]]^3} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the factor of 6 from the numerator entirely.
This uses \( b^2 \) instead of \( b^3 \) in the denominator.
This uses 3 instead of 6 in the numerator (forgetting the full cubic-integral coefficient).
This is the value of the integral itself, i.e. \( 1/c \), not \( c \).
This doubles the correct numerator.
This forgets to cube \( b \), using \( 6/b \) instead of \( 6/b^3 \).

stem: For which value of the constant \( c \) is \( f(x) = c\,e^{-\frac{[[kn]]}{[[kd]]} x} \) a valid probability density function on the interval \( x \ge 0 \) (and \( f(x) = 0 \) for \( x < 0 \))?
solution: A density on \( [0,\infty) \) must integrate to 1: \( \int_0^{\infty} c\,e^{-kx}\,dx = \frac{c}{k} = 1 \) where \( k = \frac{[[kn]]}{[[kd]]} \), so \( c = k = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This inverts the rate, using \( 1/k \) instead of \( k \).
This squares the rate instead of using it directly.
This uses \( 1/k^2 \), confusing this with a variance-type formula.
This doubles the correct rate.
This adds 1 to the correct rate.
This subtracts 1 from the correct rate.

stem: For which value of the constant \( c \) is \( f(x) = \dfrac{c}{x^{2}} \) a valid probability density function on the interval \( x \ge [[b]] \) (and \( f(x) = 0 \) for \( x < [[b]] \))?
solution: A density on \( [b,\infty) \) must integrate to 1: \( \int_{[[b]]}^{\infty} \frac{c}{x^2}\,dx = \frac{c}{[[b]]} = 1 \), so \( c = [[b]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This inverts the answer, giving \( 1/b \) instead of \( b \).
This squares \( b \) instead of using it directly.
This is the reciprocal of the squared value.
This doubles the correct value.
This is off by one below the correct value.
This is off by one above the correct value.
